mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior three months signals imminent policy easing, lowering discount rates and lifting equity valuations, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline when FEDFUNDS declines for 3 consecutive months, or if the sign prediction is wrong in more than 55% of such windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE by at least 0.3 percentage points over three months indicates labor-market deterioration that will transmit to lower earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the mean forward return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and INDPRO both expand over the prior month, the market is observing synchronized demand and production strength, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-positive condition does not outperform each proxy alone, or if the conditional 5-day forward return is not positive in at least 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO over three months signals weakening industrial supply activity and rising recession risk, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month INDPRO declines are followed by nonnegative 60-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect disappears in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A housing downturn signaled by HOUST falling below its prior 6-month average predicts weaker credit creation and consumer demand, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-breakdown events fail to precede negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average return after the trigger is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy restraint increases and equity multiples compress, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days should be negative.
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS combination does not improve predictive power over either series alone, or if triggered instances yield positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series in the presence of a rising UNRATE identifies labor-market contraction beyond seasonal noise, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor signal fails to outperform either PAYEMS or UNRATE alone, or if the 5-day forward return is not negative in at least 55% of joint-signal instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-expansion regime, strong payroll growth together with stable unemployment indicates resilient consumer demand and earnings momentum, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE does not rise
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not generate positive 20-day returns more often than the unconditional rate, or if returns turn negative when payroll gains coincide with stable unemployment.
